content:
# Exodus Chapter 25 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~46v–47r (PDF pp. 91–93); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 67–68 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_025_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_025_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_025_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_025_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE TERUMAH INSTRUCTIONS:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 25 in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter opens the massive tabernacle instruction block (chs. 25–31), delivered during Moses' forty days on the mountain (24:18). The Hebrew term terumah ("contribution, heave-offering") gives the parashah its name. The chapter moves from the general collection of materials (vv. 1–9), to the three most sacred furnishings in ascending theological importance: the Ark of the Covenant (vv. 10–22), the Table of the Bread of the Presence (vv. 23–30), and the Golden Lampstand (vv. 31–40). The keyword tavnit ("pattern, model") in vv. 9 and 40 frames the entire section, establishing that the earthly tabernacle replicates a heavenly archetype. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 67–68) presents the LXX in continuous uncial script; the LXX versification aligns with the MT throughout, though the LXX frequently renders Hebrew technical terms with Greek architectural and liturgical equivalents.

---

## The Offering for the Tabernacle (vv. 1–9)

**1** And YHWH spoke [va-yedabber YHWH] to [el] Moses [Mosheh], saying [le'mor]:

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְדַבֵּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה לֵּאמֹר. **Key terms:** וַיְדַבֵּר (va-yedabber) = piel imperfect of דבר (davar, "to speak") — the formal speech-introduction formula that opens each new divine instruction block. This speech occurs on the mountain, within the cloud of 24:18. LXX: καὶ ἐλάλησεν κύριος πρὸς Μωυσῆν λέγων — λαλέω ("speak") for va-yedabber.

**2** "Speak [dabber] to [el] the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el] and let them take [ve-yiqkhu] for me [li] a contribution [terumah]; from [me-et] every [kol] man [ish] whose [asher] heart [libbo] moves him [yiddevennu], you shall take [tiqkhu] my contribution [et-terumati].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads דַּבֵּר אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וְיִקְחוּ־לִי תְּרוּמָה מֵאֵת כָּל־אִישׁ אֲשֶׁר יִדְּבֶנּוּ לִבּוֹ תִּקְחוּ אֶת־תְּרוּמָתִי. **Key terms:** תְּרוּמָה (terumah) = "contribution, heave-offering, that which is lifted up" — from רום (rum, "to be high, to lift"); the offering is conceptually "raised" or "set apart" for YHWH. יִדְּבֶנּוּ (yiddevennu) = qal imperfect of נדב (nadav, "to impel, move voluntarily") + 3ms suffix — "his heart moves/impels him"; the offering is voluntary, not taxed. לִבּוֹ (libbo) = "his heart" — the seat of will, not mere emotion. Note the shift from plural "let them take" to singular "every man whose heart moves him" — the communal act arises from individual willing. LXX: καὶ λάβετέ μοι ἀπαρχάς — ἀπαρχή ("firstfruits, first-portion") for terumah, a significant interpretive choice that imports the firstfruits concept.

**3** And this [ve-zot] is the contribution [ha-terumah] which [asher] you shall take [tiqkhu] from them [me-ittam]: gold [zahav] and silver [va-khesef] and bronze [u-nekhoshet],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְזֹאת הַתְּרוּמָה אֲשֶׁר תִּקְחוּ מֵאִתָּם זָהָב וָכֶסֶף וּנְחֹשֶׁת. **Key terms:** זָהָב (zahav) = "gold"; כֶּסֶף (khesef) = "silver"; נְחֹשֶׁת (nekhoshet) = "bronze, copper" — the three metals listed in descending value. The metals correspond to zones of holiness in the tabernacle: gold for the Holy of Holies, silver for structural bases, bronze for the outer court. LXX: χρυσίον καὶ ἀργύριον καὶ χαλκόν — standard equivalents.

**4** and blue [u-tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and fine linen [ve-shesh] and goat hair [ve-izzim],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּתְכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ וְעִזִּים. **Key terms:** תְּכֵלֶת (tekhelet) = "blue-violet" — a dye extracted from the murex snail (Bolinus brandaris); the color of royalty and heaven. אַרְגָּמָן (argaman) = "purple" — a reddish-purple dye, also from murex species. תּוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי (tola'at shani) = "worm of scarlet" — i.e., crimson dye from the kermes insect (Kermes vermilio); literally "the crimson worm." שֵׁשׁ (shesh) = "fine linen" — a loan-word, likely from Egyptian šś; the finest quality linen. עִזִּים (izzim) = "goats" — i.e., goat hair for weaving. LXX: καὶ ὑάκινθον καὶ πορφύραν καὶ κόκκινον διπλοῦν καὶ βύσσον κεκλωσμένην καὶ τρίχας αἰγείας — ὑάκινθος ("hyacinth") for tekhelet; κόκκινον διπλοῦν ("double-scarlet") for tola'at shani; βύσσος κεκλωσμένη ("spun linen") for shesh.

**5** and skins of rams [ve-orot eilim] made red [me'oddamim] and skins of takhash [ve-orot tekhashim] and acacia wood [va-atsei shittim],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעֹרֹת אֵילִם מְאָדָּמִים וְעֹרֹת תְּחָשִׁים וַעֲצֵי שִׁטִּים. **Key terms:** עֹרֹת אֵילִם מְאָדָּמִים (orot eilim me'oddamim) = "skins of rams reddened" — ram skins dyed red, used for the tabernacle's outer covering. תְּחָשִׁים (tekhashim) = "takhash" — the identity of this animal is uncertain; proposals include dugong, dolphin, seal, or a fine leather type. The term is left untranslated. שִׁטִּים (shittim) = "acacia" — Acacia tortilis or Acacia raddiana, the primary timber of the Sinai desert. LXX: καὶ δέρματα κριῶν ἠρυθροδανωμένα καὶ δέρματα ὑακίνθινα καὶ ξύλα ἄσηπτα — ὑακίνθινα ("hyacinth-colored") for tekhashim, a color interpretation; ἄσηπτα ("incorruptible") for shittim, describing the wood's quality rather than its species.

**6** oil [shemen] for the light [la-ma'or], spices [besamim] for the oil [le-shemen] of anointing [ha-mishkhah] and for the incense [ve-liqtoret] of fragrances [ha-sammim],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שֶׁמֶן לַמָּאֹר בְּשָׂמִים לְשֶׁמֶן הַמִּשְׁחָה וְלִקְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים. **Key terms:** שֶׁמֶן לַמָּאֹר (shemen la-ma'or) = "oil for the luminary/light" — pure olive oil for the lampstand. מִשְׁחָה (mishkhah) = "anointing" — from משׁח (mashakh, "to anoint"); the root of mashiakh ("messiah, anointed one"). קְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים (qetoret ha-sammim) = "incense of the fragrances/spices" — qetoret from קטר (qatar, "to burn, send up smoke"); sammim = "aromatics, fragrant spices." LXX: καὶ ἔλαιον εἰς τὴν φαῦσιν, θυμιάματα εἰς τὸ ἔλαιον τῆς χρίσεως καὶ εἰς τὴν σύνθεσιν τοῦ θυμιάματος — θυμίαμα ("incense") for qetoret.

**7** onyx [shoham] stones [avnei] and stones [ve-avnei] of setting [millu'im] for the ephod [la-efod] and for the breastpiece [ve-la-khoshen].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אַבְנֵי־שֹׁהַם וְאַבְנֵי מִלֻּאִים לָאֵפֹד וְלַחֹשֶׁן. **Key terms:** שֹׁהַם (shoham) = "onyx" (or "lapis lazuli, carnelian") — the exact identification is uncertain. מִלֻּאִים (millu'im) = "settings, fillings" — from מלא (male, "to fill"); stones set into a mounting. אֵפֹד (efod) = "ephod" — the priestly garment; left untranslated as a technical term. חֹשֶׁן (khoshen) = "breastpiece" — the decision-breastpiece worn over the ephod, containing the Urim and Thummim. LXX: καὶ λίθους σαρδίου καὶ λίθους εἰς τὴν γλυφήν — σάρδιον ("sardius") for shoham; γλυφή ("engraving, carving") for millu'im.

**8** And let them make [ve-asu] for me [li] a sanctuary [miqdash], and I will dwell [ve-shakhanti] in their midst [be-tokham].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשׂוּ לִי מִקְדָּשׁ וְשָׁכַנְתִּי בְּתוֹכָם. **Key terms:** מִקְדָּשׁ (miqdash) = "sanctuary, holy place" — from קדשׁ (qadash, "to be holy, set apart"); a place made holy by divine presence. וְשָׁכַנְתִּי (ve-shakhanti) = qal perfect 1cs of שׁכן (shakhan, "to dwell, tabernacle") — "and I will dwell/tabernacle." This is the theological center of the tabernacle project: YHWH will dwell among his people. Note: "in their midst" (be-tokham), not "in it" — the goal is not a building but a dwelling among persons. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις μοι ἁγίασμα καὶ ὀφθήσομαι ἐν ὑμῖν — ὀφθήσομαι ("I will be seen/appear") for ve-shakhanti, a striking divergence: the LXX substitutes visible appearing for indwelling.

**9** According to all [ke-khol] that [asher] I [ani] am showing you [mar'eh otkha], the pattern [et tavnit] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] and the pattern [ve-et tavnit] of all [kol] its vessels [kelav], and thus [ve-khen] you shall make [ta'asu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כְּכֹל אֲשֶׁר אֲנִי מַרְאֶה אוֹתְךָ אֵת תַּבְנִית הַמִּשְׁכָּן וְאֵת תַּבְנִית כָּל־כֵּלָיו וְכֵן תַּעֲשׂוּ. **Key terms:** תַּבְנִית (tavnit) = "pattern, plan, model" — from בנה (banah, "to build"); the architectural blueprint or heavenly prototype. This term recurs in v. 40, framing the entire chapter. מִשְׁכָּן (mishkan) = "tabernacle, dwelling-place" — from שׁכן (shakhan, "to dwell"); cognate with ve-shakhanti in v. 8. כֵּלָיו (kelav) = "its vessels, its implements" — from כלי (keli, "vessel, utensil, implement"). מַרְאֶה (mar'eh) = hiphil participle of ראה (ra'ah, "to see") — "causing to see, showing"; God visually displays the pattern to Moses. LXX: κατὰ πάντα ὅσα ἐγώ σοι δεικνύω ἐν τῷ ὄρει τὸ παράδειγμα τῆς σκηνῆς — παράδειγμα ("example, model") for tavnit; σκηνή ("tent") for mishkan. The LXX adds ἐν τῷ ὄρει ("on the mountain"), making the location explicit.

---

## The Ark of the Covenant (vv. 10–22)

**10** And they shall make [ve-asu] an ark [aron] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood; two cubits [ammatayim] and a half [va-khetsi] its length [orkho], and a cubit [ve-ammah] and a half [va-khetsi] its width [rokhbo], and a cubit [ve-ammah] and a half [va-khetsi] its height [qomato].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשׂוּ אֲרוֹן עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים אַמָּתַיִם וָחֵצִי אָרְכּוֹ וְאַמָּה וָחֵצִי רָחְבּוֹ וְאַמָּה וָחֵצִי קֹמָתוֹ. **Key terms:** אֲרוֹן (aron) = "ark, chest, box" — from an uncertain root; the container for the tablets of the testimony. The dimensions (2.5 x 1.5 x 1.5 cubits, approximately 3.75 x 2.25 x 2.25 feet) are given in half-cubits, which the Talmud (Yoma 21a) notes: every dimension is "broken" — incomplete — pointing to the humility required of Torah scholars. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις κιβωτὸν μαρτυρίου ἐκ ξύλων ἀσήπτων — κιβωτός ("chest, box") for aron; the LXX adds μαρτυρίου ("of testimony"), importing the full title already.

**11** And you shall overlay it [ve-tsippita oto] with pure [tahor] gold [zahav]; from inside [mi-bayit] and from outside [u-mi-khuts] you shall overlay it [tetsappennu], and you shall make [ve-asita] upon it [alav] a molding [zer] of gold [zahav] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתוֹ זָהָב טָהוֹר מִבַּיִת וּמִחוּץ תְּצַפֶּנּוּ וְעָשִׂיתָ עָלָיו זֵר זָהָב סָבִיב. **Key terms:** וְצִפִּיתָ (ve-tsippita) = piel perfect of צפה (tsafah, "to overlay, plate") — "and you shall overlay." טָהוֹר (tahor) = "pure, clean" — from טהר (tahar, "to be clean/pure"). מִבַּיִת וּמִחוּץ (mi-bayit u-mi-khuts) = "from inside and from outside" — the ark is gold inside and out, with acacia wood as the core. זֵר (zer) = "molding, border, crown" — a decorative rim or crown-molding around the top edge. LXX: καὶ καταχρυσώσεις αὐτὴν χρυσίῳ καθαρῷ — καταχρυσόω ("gild, overlay with gold") for ve-tsippita.

**12** And you shall cast [ve-yatsaqta] for it [lo] four [arba] rings [tabb'ot] of gold [zahav], and you shall put [ve-natatta] [them] upon [al] its four [arba] feet [pa'amotav], and two [u-shtei] rings [tabb'ot] upon [al] its one [ha-ekhad] side [tsal'o] and two [u-shtei] rings [tabb'ot] upon [al] its second [ha-shenit] side [tsal'o].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְיָצַקְתָּ לּוֹ אַרְבַּע טַבְּעֹת זָהָב וְנָתַתָּה עַל אַרְבַּע פַּעֲמֹתָיו וּשְׁתֵּי טַבָּעֹת עַל־צַלְעוֹ הָאֶחָת וּשְׁתֵּי טַבָּעֹת עַל־צַלְעוֹ הַשֵּׁנִית. **Key terms:** וְיָצַקְתָּ (ve-yatsaqta) = qal perfect of יצק (yatsaq, "to pour, cast") — cast metal rings. טַבְּעֹת (tabb'ot) = "rings" — from טבע (tava, "to sink, impress"); signet-like rings. פַּעֲמֹתָיו (pa'amotav) = "its feet, its legs" — from פעם (pa'am, "foot, step, time"); the four corner-feet of the ark. צַלְעוֹ (tsal'o) = "its side, its rib" — from צלע (tsela, "side, rib"). LXX: καὶ ἐλάσεις αὐτῇ τέσσαρας δακτυλίους χρυσοῦς — δακτύλιος ("ring") for tabb'ot; ἐλαύνω ("hammer, forge") for ve-yatsaqta.

**13** And you shall make [ve-asita] poles [baddei] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood, and you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] them [otam] with gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ בַדֵּי עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתָם זָהָב. **Key terms:** בַדֵּי (baddei) = "poles of, staves of" — from בד (bad, "branch, pole, limb"); the carrying poles inserted through the rings. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις ἀναφορεῖς ξύλα ἄσηπτα — ἀναφορεύς ("carrying pole") for baddei.

**14** And you shall bring [ve-heveta] the poles [et-ha-baddim] into [be-] the rings [tabb'ot] upon [al] the sides [tsal'ot] of the ark [ha-aron] to carry [la-set] the ark [et-ha-aron] with them [ba-hem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהֵבֵאתָ אֶת־הַבַּדִּים בַּטַּבָּעֹת עַל צַלְעֹת הָאָרֹן לָשֵׂאת אֶת־הָאָרֹן בָּהֶם. **Key terms:** וְהֵבֵאתָ (ve-heveta) = hiphil perfect of בוא (bo, "to come, enter") — "and you shall cause to enter, bring in." לָשֵׂאת (la-set) = qal infinitive construct of נשׂא (nasa, "to lift, carry") — "to carry." The ark is designed for portability; it must be carried, never placed on a cart (cf. 2 Sam 6:3–7). LXX: καὶ εἰσάξεις τοὺς ἀναφορεῖς εἰς τοὺς δακτυλίους — εἰσάγω ("bring in") for ve-heveta.

**15** In the rings [be-tabb'ot] of the ark [ha-aron] the poles [ha-baddim] shall be [yihyu]; they shall not be removed [lo yasuru] from it [mimmennu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads בְּטַבְּעֹת הָאָרֹן יִהְיוּ הַבַּדִּים לֹא יָסֻרוּ מִמֶּנּוּ. **Key terms:** לֹא יָסֻרוּ (lo yasuru) = qal imperfect of סור (sur, "to turn aside, remove") — "they shall not be removed." The poles remain permanently in the rings — the ark is always ready for travel, always portable, never settled. LXX: ἐν τοῖς δακτυλίοις τῆς κιβωτοῦ ἔσονται οἱ ἀναφορεῖς ἀκίνητοι — ἀκίνητος ("immovable") for lo yasuru.

**16** And you shall put [ve-natatta] into [el] the ark [ha-aron] the testimony [et-ha-edut] which [asher] I will give [etten] to you [elekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ אֶל־הָאָרֹן אֵת הָעֵדֻת אֲשֶׁר אֶתֵּן אֵלֶיךָ. **Key terms:** הָעֵדֻת (ha-edut) = "the testimony" — from עוד (ud, "to testify, witness"); the stone tablets as the covenant witness-document. The ark's primary function is as a container for the tablets; it is the "ark of the testimony" (aron ha-edut). LXX: καὶ ἐμβαλεῖς εἰς τὴν κιβωτὸν τὰ μαρτύρια ἃ ἂν δῶ σοι — μαρτύρια ("testimonies") for ha-edut.

**17** And you shall make [ve-asita] a mercy seat [kapporet] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav]; two cubits [ammatayim] and a half [va-khetsi] its length [orkah], and a cubit [ve-ammah] and a half [va-khetsi] its width [rokhbah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ כַפֹּרֶת זָהָב טָהוֹר אַמָּתַיִם וָחֵצִי אָרְכָּהּ וְאַמָּה וָחֵצִי רָחְבָּהּ. **Key terms:** כַּפֹּרֶת (kapporet) = "mercy seat, atonement cover" — from כפר (kafar, "to cover, atone, ransom"); the gold lid of the ark where atonement is made. This is not merely a "lid" but the place of propitiation — where the blood of Yom Kippur is sprinkled (Lev 16:14–15). The dimensions match the ark exactly, serving as its cover. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις ἱλαστήριον ἐπίθεμα χρυσίου καθαροῦ — ἱλαστήριον ("place of propitiation") for kapporet; ἐπίθεμα ("lid, cover") is added as explanation. This LXX term ἱλαστήριον is cited by Paul in Romans 3:25.

**18** And you shall make [ve-asita] two [shnei] cherubim [keruvim] of gold [zahav]; hammered work [miqshah] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam] from the two [mi-shnei] ends [qetsot] of the mercy seat [ha-kapporet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ שְׁנַיִם כְּרֻבִים זָהָב מִקְשָׁה תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם מִשְּׁנֵי קְצוֹת הַכַּפֹּרֶת. **Key terms:** כְּרֻבִים (keruvim) = "cherubim" — angelic guardian figures; the etymology is debated (possibly related to Akkadian karibu, "intercessor"). מִקְשָׁה (miqshah) = "hammered work, beaten work" — from קשׁה (qashah, "to be hard"); the cherubim are hammered from a single piece of gold, not cast or assembled. קְצוֹת (qetsot) = "ends, extremities" — from קצה (qatseh, "end"). LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις δύο χερουβιμ χρυσᾶ τορευτά — τορευτός ("turned on a lathe, wrought") for miqshah.

**19** And make [va-aseh] one [ekhad] cherub [keruv] from this [mi-zeh] end [mi-qatseh] and one [ekhad] cherub [keruv] from this [mi-zeh] end [mi-qatseh]; from [min] the mercy seat [ha-kapporet] you shall make [ta'asu] the cherubim [et-ha-keruvim] upon [al] its two [shnei] ends [qetsotav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַעֲשֵׂה כְּרוּב אֶחָד מִקָּצָה מִזֶּה וּכְרוּב־אֶחָד מִקָּצָה מִזֶּה מִן־הַכַּפֹּרֶת תַּעֲשׂוּ אֶת־הַכְּרֻבִים עַל־שְׁנֵי קְצוֹתָיו. **Key terms:** מִן־הַכַּפֹּרֶת (min-ha-kapporet) = "from the mercy seat" — the cherubim are hammered out of the kapporet itself; they are organically one piece with it, not attached separately. The preposition min ("from, out of") emphasizes their integral unity with the atonement cover. LXX: χερουβ εἷς ἐκ τοῦ κλίτους τούτου καὶ χερουβ εἷς ἐκ τοῦ κλίτους τοῦ δευτέρου — κλίτος ("side") for qatseh.

**20** And the cherubim [ve-hayu ha-keruvim] shall be spreading [porsei] their wings [kenafayim] upward [le-ma'lah], covering [sokhekhim] with their wings [be-kanfeihem] over [al] the mercy seat [ha-kapporet], and their faces [u-feneihem] [shall be] each [ish] toward [el] his brother [akhiv]; toward [el] the mercy seat [ha-kapporet] the faces [penei] of the cherubim [ha-keruvim] shall be [yihyu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיוּ הַכְּרֻבִים פֹּרְשֵׂי כְנָפַיִם לְמַעְלָה סֹכְכִים בְּכַנְפֵיהֶם עַל־הַכַּפֹּרֶת וּפְנֵיהֶם אִישׁ אֶל־אָחִיו אֶל־הַכַּפֹּרֶת יִהְיוּ פְּנֵי הַכְּרֻבִים. **Key terms:** פֹּרְשֵׂי (porsei) = qal participle of פרשׂ (paras, "to spread out, stretch") — "spreading out." סֹכְכִים (sokhekhim) = qal participle of סכך (sakhakh, "to cover, screen, protect") — "covering, screening." אִישׁ אֶל־אָחִיו (ish el-akhiv) = "each to his brother" — i.e., facing one another. The cherubim look toward each other and downward toward the kapporet — toward the place of atonement. LXX: ἔσονται οἱ χερουβιμ ἐκτείνοντες τὰς πτέρυγας ἐπάνωθεν — ἐκτείνω ("stretch out") for porsei.

**21** And you shall put [ve-natatta] the mercy seat [et-ha-kapporet] upon [al] the ark [ha-aron] from above [mi-lema'lah], and into [ve-el] the ark [ha-aron] you shall put [titten] the testimony [et-ha-edut] which [asher] I will give [etten] to you [elekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ אֶת־הַכַּפֹּרֶת עַל־הָאָרֹן מִלְמָעְלָה וְאֶל־הָאָרֹן תִּתֵּן אֶת־הָעֵדֻת אֲשֶׁר אֶתֵּן אֵלֶיךָ. **Key terms:** מִלְמָעְלָה (mi-lema'lah) = "from above, on top" — the kapporet sits atop the ark, completing the ensemble. This verse recapitulates v. 16, framing the ark instructions with the testimony's placement. LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσεις τὸ ἱλαστήριον ἐπὶ τὴν κιβωτὸν ἄνωθεν — ἄνωθεν ("from above") for mi-lema'lah.

**22** And I will meet with you [ve-no'adti lekha] there [sham], and I will speak [ve-dibbarti] with you [ittkha] from above [me-al] the mercy seat [ha-kapporet], from between [mi-bein] the two [shnei] cherubim [ha-keruvim] which [asher] are upon [al] the ark [aron] of the testimony [ha-edut], all [et kol] that [asher] I will command you [atsavveh otkha] to [el] the Sons of Israel [benei Yisra'el].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנוֹעַדְתִּי לְךָ שָׁם וְדִבַּרְתִּי אִתְּךָ מֵעַל הַכַּפֹּרֶת מִבֵּין שְׁנֵי הַכְּרֻבִים אֲשֶׁר עַל־אֲרוֹן הָעֵדֻת אֵת כָּל־אֲשֶׁר אֲצַוֶּה אוֹתְךָ אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל. **Key terms:** וְנוֹעַדְתִּי (ve-no'adti) = niphal perfect 1cs of יעד (ya'ad, "to appoint, meet by appointment") — "and I will meet by appointment with you." The kapporet is the appointed meeting-place between YHWH and Moses — the specific locus of divine communication. מִבֵּין שְׁנֵי הַכְּרֻבִים (mi-bein shnei ha-keruvim) = "from between the two cherubim" — YHWH's voice emerges from the space between the cherubim, the invisible throne above the kapporet. This verse establishes the ark-complex as the functional equivalent of Sinai: the place where God speaks. LXX: καὶ γνωσθήσομαί σοι ἐκεῖθεν καὶ λαλήσω σοι ἄνωθεν τοῦ ἱλαστηρίου ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν δύο χερουβιμ — γνωσθήσομαι ("I will make myself known") for ve-no'adti, shifting from "meeting" to "being known."

---

## The Table of the Bread of the Presence (vv. 23–30)

**23** And you shall make [ve-asita] a table [shulkhan] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood; two [ammatayim] cubits its length [orkho], and a cubit [ve-ammah] its width [rokhbo], and a cubit [ve-ammah] and a half [va-khetsi] its height [qomato].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ שֻׁלְחָן עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים אַמָּתַיִם אָרְכּוֹ וְאַמָּה רָחְבּוֹ וְאַמָּה וָחֵצִי קֹמָתוֹ. **Key terms:** שֻׁלְחָן (shulkhan) = "table" — from שׁלח (shalakh, "to send"); a table is literally "that which is spread out." The table dimensions (2 x 1 x 1.5 cubits, approximately 3 x 1.5 x 2.25 feet) are smaller than the ark in length but taller. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις τράπεζαν χρυσῆν ξύλων ἀσήπτων — τράπεζα ("table") for shulkhan; the LXX adds χρυσῆν ("golden") prematurely.

**24** And you shall overlay it [ve-tsippita oto] with pure [tahor] gold [zahav], and you shall make [ve-asita] for it [lo] a molding [zer] of gold [zahav] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתוֹ זָהָב טָהוֹר וְעָשִׂיתָ לּוֹ זֵר זָהָב סָבִיב. LXX: καὶ χρυσώσεις αὐτὴν χρυσίῳ καθαρῷ καὶ ποιήσεις αὐτῇ στρεπτὸν κυμάτιον χρυσοῦν κύκλῳ — κυμάτιον ("molding, wave-pattern") for zer.

**25** And you shall make [ve-asita] for it [lo] a border [misgeret] of a handbreadth [tefakh] all around [saviv], and you shall make [ve-asita] a molding [zer] of gold [zahav] for its border [le-misgereto] all around [saviv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ לּוֹ מִסְגֶּרֶת טֹפַח סָבִיב וְעָשִׂיתָ זֵר־זָהָב לְמִסְגַּרְתּוֹ סָבִיב. **Key terms:** מִסְגֶּרֶת (misgeret) = "border, frame, rim" — from סגר (sagar, "to close, shut"); an enclosing frame. טֹפַח (tefakh) = "a handbreadth" — approximately 3 inches (7.5 cm); the width of the frame. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις αὐτῇ στεφάνην παλαιστοῦ κύκλῳ — παλαιστή ("handbreadth, palm-width") for tefakh.

**26** And you shall make [ve-asita] for it [lo] four [arba] rings [tabb'ot] of gold [zahav], and you shall put [ve-natatta] the rings [et-ha-tabb'ot] upon [al] the four [arba] corners [ha-pe'ot] which [asher] are at [le-] its four [arba] legs [raglav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ לּוֹ אַרְבַּע טַבְּעֹת זָהָב וְנָתַתָּ אֶת־הַטַּבָּעֹת עַל אַרְבַּע הַפֵּאֹת אֲשֶׁר לְאַרְבַּע רַגְלָיו. **Key terms:** פֵּאֹת (pe'ot) = "corners, edges" — from פאה (pe'ah, "corner, side, edge"). רַגְלָיו (raglav) = "its legs, its feet" — from רגל (regel, "foot, leg"). LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις τέσσαρας δακτυλίους χρυσοῦς — the LXX aligns closely with the MT here.

**27** Close to [le-ummat] the border [ha-misgeret] the rings [ha-tabb'ot] shall be [tihyena], as holders [le-vatim] for the poles [le-baddim] to carry [la-set] the table [et-ha-shulkhan].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לְעֻמַּת הַמִּסְגֶּרֶת תִּהְיֶיןָ הַטַּבָּעֹת לְבָתִּים לְבַדִּים לָשֵׂאת אֶת־הַשֻּׁלְחָן. **Key terms:** לְעֻמַּת (le-ummat) = "close to, alongside, corresponding to" — the rings are positioned near the border-frame. לְבָתִּים (le-vatim) = "as holders, as housings" — from בית (bayit, "house"); the rings serve as "houses" for the poles. LXX: ἔσονται αἱ ψαλίδες εἰς θήκας τοῖς ἀναφορεῦσιν — θήκη ("sheath, holder") for vatim.

**28** And you shall make [ve-asita] the poles [et-ha-baddim] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood, and you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] them [otam] with gold [zahav], and the table [ha-shulkhan] shall be carried [ve-nissa] with them [ba-hem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־הַבַּדִּים עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתָם זָהָב וְנִשָּׂא־בָם אֶת־הַשֻּׁלְחָן. **Key terms:** וְנִשָּׂא (ve-nissa) = niphal perfect of נשׂא (nasa, "to carry, lift") — "and it shall be carried"; the passive voice indicates the table is the object carried. LXX: καὶ ἀρθήσεται ἐν αὐτοῖς ἡ τράπεζα — αἴρω ("lift, carry") for ve-nissa.

**29** And you shall make [ve-asita] its dishes [qe'arotav] and its ladles [ve-khappotav] and its jars [ve-qesotav] and its bowls [u-menaqiyyotav] which [asher] will be poured out with [yussakh] them [ba-hen]; pure [tahor] gold [zahav] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ קְעָרֹתָיו וְכַפֹּתָיו וּקְשׂוֹתָיו וּמְנַקִּיֹּתָיו אֲשֶׁר יֻסַּךְ בָּהֵן זָהָב טָהוֹר תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם. **Key terms:** קְעָרֹתָיו (qe'arotav) = "its dishes, its plates" — from קערה (qe'arah, "dish, plate"). כַּפֹּתָיו (khappotav) = "its ladles, its spoons" — from כף (kaf, "palm, hollow of hand"); palm-shaped scoops. קְשׂוֹתָיו (qesotav) = "its jars, its flagons" — vessels for drink offerings. מְנַקִּיֹּתָיו (menaqiyyotav) = "its bowls, its pitchers" — from נקה (naqah, "to pour out"); vessels for pouring libations. יֻסַּךְ (yussakh) = hophal imperfect of נסך (nasakh, "to pour out") — "will be poured out"; referring to libation vessels. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις τὰ τρυβλία αὐτῆς καὶ τὰς θυΐσκας καὶ τὰ σπονδεῖα καὶ τοὺς κυάθους — σπονδεῖον ("libation vessel") for qesotav.

**30** And you shall set [ve-natatta] upon [al] the table [ha-shulkhan] the bread [lekhem] of the presence [panim] before me [le-fanai] continually [tamid].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ עַל־הַשֻּׁלְחָן לֶחֶם פָּנִים לְפָנַי תָּמִיד. **Key terms:** לֶחֶם פָּנִים (lekhem panim) = "bread of the face/presence" — literally "face-bread"; the twelve loaves set before YHWH's face as a perpetual offering (Lev 24:5–9). תָּמִיד (tamid) = "continually, perpetually" — the bread is never absent from the table; it is renewed every Sabbath. The bread represents Israel's ongoing communion with YHWH — sustenance in the divine presence. LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσεις ἐπὶ τὴν τράπεζαν ἄρτους ἐνωπίους ἐναντίον μου διὰ παντός — ἄρτοι ἐνώπιοι ("loaves of the presence") for lekhem panim; διὰ παντός ("through all [time]") for tamid.

---

## The Golden Lampstand (vv. 31–40)

**31** And you shall make [ve-asita] a lampstand [menorat] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav]; hammered work [miqshah] the lampstand [ha-menorah] shall be made [te'aseh]; its base [yerekah] and its shaft [ve-qanah], its cups [gevi'eha], its knobs [kaftoreha], and its blossoms [u-ferakheha] shall be [yihyu] from it [mimmennah] — [that is,] of one piece with it.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ מְנֹרַת זָהָב טָהוֹר מִקְשָׁה תֵּעָשֶׂה הַמְּנוֹרָה יְרֵכָהּ וְקָנָהּ גְּבִיעֶיהָ כַּפְתֹּרֶיהָ וּפְרָחֶיהָ מִמֶּנָּה יִהְיוּ. **Key terms:** מְנוֹרָה (menorah) = "lampstand" — from נר (ner, "lamp"); the seven-branched lampstand. מִקְשָׁה (miqshah) = "hammered work" — the entire lampstand is beaten from one talent of gold (v. 39). יְרֵכָהּ (yerekah) = "its thigh, its base" — from ירך (yarekh, "thigh, base"); the pedestal. קָנָהּ (qanah) = "its shaft, its reed" — from קנה (qaneh, "reed, stalk"); the central stem. גְּבִיעֶיהָ (gevi'eha) = "its cups, its goblets" — from גביע (gavia, "cup, goblet"); almond-blossom-shaped cups. כַּפְתֹּרֶיהָ (kaftoreha) = "its knobs, its bulbs" — ornamental knobs. פְרָחֶיהָ (ferakheha) = "its blossoms, its flowers" — from פרח (perakh, "blossom"). מִמֶּנָּה (mimmennah) = "from it" — all parts are integral, hammered from one piece. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις λυχνίαν ἐκ χρυσίου καθαροῦ τορευτήν — λυχνία ("lampstand") for menorah; τορευτή ("wrought, turned") for miqshah.

**32** And six [ve-shishah] branches [qanim] going out [yotse'im] from its sides [mi-tsiddeyha]: three [shloshah] branches [qenei] of the lampstand [menorah] from its one [mi-tsiddah ha-ekhad] side, and three [u-shloshah] branches [qenei] of the lampstand [menorah] from its second [mi-tsiddah ha-sheni] side.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשִׁשָּׁה קָנִים יֹצְאִים מִצִּדֶּיהָ שְׁלֹשָׁה קְנֵי מְנֹרָה מִצִּדָּהּ הָאֶחָד וּשְׁלֹשָׁה קְנֵי מְנֹרָה מִצִּדָּהּ הַשֵּׁנִי. **Key terms:** קָנִים (qanim) = "branches, reeds" — plural of קנה (qaneh); six lateral branches plus the central shaft makes seven. יֹצְאִים (yotse'im) = qal participle of יצא (yatsa, "to go out") — "going out, extending." מִצִּדֶּיהָ (mi-tsiddeyha) = "from its sides" — from צד (tsad, "side"). LXX: ἓξ καλαμίσκοι ἐκπορευόμενοι ἐκ πλαγίων — καλαμίσκος ("small reed") for qanim.

**33** Three [shloshah] cups [gevi'im] shaped like almond blossoms [meshukaddim] on the one [ba-qaneh ha-ekhad] branch — a knob [kaftor] and a blossom [va-ferakh]; and three [u-shloshah] cups [gevi'im] shaped like almond blossoms [meshukaddim] on the other [ba-qaneh ha-ekhad] branch — a knob [kaftor] and a blossom [va-ferakh]; so [ken] for the six [le-sheshet] branches [ha-qanim] going out [ha-yotse'im] from [min] the lampstand [ha-menorah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שְׁלֹשָׁה גְבִעִים מְשֻׁקָּדִים בַּקָּנֶה הָאֶחָד כַּפְתֹּר וָפֶרַח וּשְׁלֹשָׁה גְבִעִים מְשֻׁקָּדִים בַּקָּנֶה הָאֶחָד כַּפְתֹּר וָפֶרַח כֵּן לְשֵׁשֶׁת הַקָּנִים הַיֹּצְאִים מִן־הַמְּנֹרָה. **Key terms:** מְשֻׁקָּדִים (meshukaddim) = pual participle of שׁקד (shaqad, "almond; to watch, be wakeful") — "made in almond-blossom shape"; the almond (shaqed) is the first tree to bloom in spring, and its name puns on "watchful" (cf. Jer 1:11–12). Each branch has three cup-knob-blossom sets. LXX: τρεῖς κρατῆρες ἐκτετυπωμένοι καρυΐσκους — κρατήρ ("mixing cup") for gevi'im; καρυΐσκος ("nut-shaped") for meshukaddim.

**34** And on the lampstand [u-va-menorah] [shall be] four [arba'ah] cups [gevi'im] shaped like almond blossoms [meshukaddim] — its knobs [kaftorehah] and its blossoms [u-ferakheha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבַמְּנֹרָה אַרְבָּעָה גְבִעִים מְשֻׁקָּדִים כַּפְתֹּרֶיהָ וּפְרָחֶיהָ. The central shaft has four almond-blossom cups (one more than each branch). LXX: καὶ ἐν τῇ λυχνίᾳ τέσσαρες κρατῆρες ἐκτετυπωμένοι καρυΐσκους — the LXX aligns with the MT.

**35** And a knob [ve-kaftor] under [takhat] the two [shnei] branches [ha-qanim] from it [mimmenah], and a knob [ve-kaftor] under [takhat] the two [shnei] branches [ha-qanim] from it [mimmenah], and a knob [ve-kaftor] under [takhat] the two [shnei] branches [ha-qanim] from it [mimmenah] — for the six [le-sheshet] branches [ha-qanim] going out [ha-yotse'im] from [min] the lampstand [ha-menorah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְכַפְתֹּר תַּחַת שְׁנֵי הַקָּנִים מִמֶּנָּה וְכַפְתֹּר תַּחַת שְׁנֵי הַקָּנִים מִמֶּנָּה וְכַפְתֹּר תַּחַת שְׁנֵי הַקָּנִים מִמֶּנָּה לְשֵׁשֶׁת הַקָּנִים הַיֹּצְאִים מִן־הַמְּנֹרָה. Three knobs on the central shaft, each at the junction where a pair of branches extends — structuring the lampstand as an organic, tree-like form. LXX: ὁ καλαμίσκος ὑπὸ τοὺς δύο καλαμίσκους ἐξ αὐτῆς — the LXX repeats the pattern three times as in the MT.

**36** Their knobs [kaftoreihem] and their branches [u-qenotam] shall be [yihyu] from it [mimmennah]; all of it [kullahh] one [ekhad] hammered work [miqshah] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כַּפְתֹּרֵיהֶם וּקְנֹתָם מִמֶּנָּה יִהְיוּ כֻּלָּהּ מִקְשָׁה אַחַת זָהָב טָהוֹר. **Key terms:** כֻּלָּהּ מִקְשָׁה אַחַת (kullahh miqshah akhat) = "all of it one hammered work" — the entire lampstand, with all its branches, cups, knobs, and blossoms, is beaten from a single piece of gold. LXX: πάντα τορευτὰ ἐξ ἑνὸς χρυσίου καθαροῦ — ἐξ ἑνός ("from one [piece]") for miqshah akhat.

**37** And you shall make [ve-asita] its lamps [et-neroteha] seven [shiv'ah], and he shall set up [ve-he'elah] its lamps [et-neroteha] and it shall give light [ve-he'ir] upon [al] the space [ever] in front of it [paneha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־נֵרֹתֶיהָ שִׁבְעָה וְהֶעֱלָה אֶת־נֵרֹתֶיהָ וְהֵאִיר עַל־עֵבֶר פָּנֶיהָ. **Key terms:** נֵרֹתֶיהָ (neroteha) = "its lamps" — from נר (ner, "lamp"); seven lamps set on the seven branches. וְהֶעֱלָה (ve-he'elah) = hiphil perfect of עלה (alah, "to go up") — "and he shall cause to go up," i.e., "he shall set up/light" the lamps. עֵבֶר פָּנֶיהָ (ever paneha) = "the space of its face," i.e., "the area in front of it." The lampstand illuminates forward, toward the table of showbread across the Holy Place. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις τοὺς λύχνους αὐτῆς ἑπτά — λύχνος ("lamp") for nerot.

**38** And its tongs [u-malqakheha] and its firepans [u-makhtoteha] [shall be] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּמַלְקָחֶיהָ וּמַחְתֹּתֶיהָ זָהָב טָהוֹר. **Key terms:** מַלְקָחֶיהָ (malqakheha) = "its tongs, its snuffers" — from לקח (laqakh, "to take"); instruments for trimming the lamp wicks. מַחְתֹּתֶיהָ (makhtoteha) = "its firepans, its trays" — from חתה (khatah, "to snatch up coals"); small pans for removing ash and charred wick material. LXX: καὶ τὰς λαβίδας αὐτῆς καὶ τὰ ὑποθέματα αὐτῆς — λαβίς ("tongs") for malqakheha; ὑπόθεμα ("support, base-tray") for makhtoteha.

**39** A talent [kikkar] of pure [tahor] gold [zahav] he shall make [ya'aseh] it [otah] with all [et kol] these [ha-kelim] vessels [ha-elleh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּכַּר זָהָב טָהוֹר יַעֲשֶׂה אֹתָהּ אֵת כָּל־הַכֵּלִים הָאֵלֶּה. **Key terms:** כִּכַּר (kikkar) = "talent, round disk" — a unit of weight approximately 75 pounds (34 kg); the entire lampstand with all its accessories is made from one talent of pure gold. LXX: τάλαντον χρυσίου καθαροῦ ποιήσεις αὐτὴν σὺν πᾶσιν τοῖς σκεύεσιν τούτοις — τάλαντον ("talent") for kikkar.

**40** And see [u-re'eh] and make [va-aseh] according to their pattern [be-tavnitam] which [asher] you [attah] are being shown [mar'eh] on the mountain [ba-har].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּרְאֵה וַעֲשֵׂה בְּתַבְנִיתָם אֲשֶׁר־אַתָּה מָרְאֶה בָּהָר. **Key terms:** וּרְאֵה (u-re'eh) = qal imperative of ראה (ra'ah, "to see") — "and see!" A command to observe carefully. בְּתַבְנִיתָם (be-tavnitam) = "according to their pattern" — tavnit recurs from v. 9, forming an inclusio around the chapter. מָרְאֶה (mar'eh) = hophal participle of ראה — "being shown, being caused to see"; Moses is the passive recipient of a divine visual revelation. The verse assumes Moses sees a heavenly archetype that the earthly tabernacle must replicate — a concept developed extensively in Hebrews 8:5. LXX: ὅρα ποιήσεις κατὰ τὸν τύπον τὸν δεδειγμένον σοι ἐν τῷ ὄρει — τύπος ("type, pattern, model") for tavnit; δείκνυμι ("show") for mar'eh.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 25 inaugurates the longest continuous divine speech in the Torah — seven chapters of tabernacle instruction (25–31) delivered during Moses' forty days within the cloud on Sinai (24:18). The chapter moves from the voluntary offering (terumah) to three sacred objects in ascending theological weight: the table, the lampstand, and supremely the ark with its kapporet. Yet the literary order begins with the ark (vv. 10–22), the innermost and holiest item, working outward — God starts from his own throne and moves toward the people.

The keyword tavnit ("pattern," vv. 9, 40) frames the chapter as an inclusio and establishes a foundational theological claim: the earthly tabernacle replicates a heavenly original. Moses is "being shown" (mar'eh, hophal) something on the mountain that he must reproduce below. The nature of this heavenly archetype — whether a literal celestial structure, a visionary blueprint, or a theological metaphor — is not specified, but the language insists on exact correspondence. The LXX renders tavnit as both παράδειγμα (v. 9) and τύπος (v. 40), the latter term becoming foundational for the typological hermeneutic of Hebrews 8:5.

The terumah of vv. 1–9 is notable for its motivational basis: "every man whose heart moves him" (asher yiddevennu libbo). The offering is not taxed or conscripted but arises from inner voluntary impulse. The verb nadav ("to move voluntarily") is the same root as Nadab, Aaron's firstborn — a possible wordplay foreshadowing the priestly family's role. The materials list (gold, silver, bronze; blue, purple, scarlet; linen, goat hair; ram skins, takhash skins; acacia wood; oil, spices, stones) encompasses mineral, vegetable, and animal kingdoms, suggesting that all creation contributes to YHWH's dwelling.

The ark instructions (vv. 10–22) culminate in the extraordinary promise of v. 22: "I will meet with you there and I will speak with you from above the kapporet, from between the two cherubim." The verb no'adti (niphal of ya'ad, "to appoint a meeting") indicates that the kapporet is an appointed meeting-place — YHWH sets the time and place of encounter. The space "between the two cherubim" is the functional Sinai within the tent: the invisible throne from which the divine voice issues. The kapporet itself (from kafar, "to atone, cover") is simultaneously lid, throne, and place of propitiation — a convergence of architectural function and atonement theology that the LXX captures with the word hilasterion, later cited by Paul (Rom 3:25) for Christ himself.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) In v. 2, the LXX renders terumah as aparche ("firstfruits"), importing an agricultural-offering concept absent from the Hebrew. (2) In v. 5, the LXX translates tekhashim as huakinthina ("hyacinth-colored"), interpreting the unknown animal as a color designation. (3) In v. 8, the LXX reads ophthosomai ("I will be seen/appear") for ve-shakhanti ("I will dwell"), replacing the theology of indwelling with theophanic visibility — a significant theological shift. (4) In v. 9, the LXX adds en to orei ("on the mountain"), making explicit what the MT leaves implicit. (5) In v. 22, the LXX reads gnosthesomai ("I will make myself known") for ve-no'adti ("I will meet by appointment"), shifting from spatial encounter to cognitive revelation. (6) Throughout, the LXX translates shittim ("acacia") as ashepta ("incorruptible wood"), describing the quality rather than the species, and consistently uses toreutos ("wrought, turned") for miqshah ("hammered work"), a difference in craft terminology.
